channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Exponential growth but for how long: DeepSeek's meteoric rise in AI-land continues unabated, fresh Similarweb data shows
item.description: Data from Similarweb shows DeepSeek is now the third most used AI tool, but is its popularity already waning?
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Huge healthcare data breach exposes over 1 million Americans' sensitive information
item.description: Community Health Center, a Connecticut-based healthcare provider, has disclosed a recent data breach affecting over 1 million people in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sony PlayStation Network outage enrages gamers around the world
item.description: Sony’s PlayStation Network (PSN) is experiencing a major outage across the world, leaving tens of thousands of gamers unable to access online services, stores and multimedia apps.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: OpenAI launches cross-country search to build data center sites for the Stargate project
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: These groups are pushing for the NFL to end facial recognition
item.description: Ahead of Super Bowl Sunday, online privacy groups Fight for the Future and the Algorithmic Justice League are reiterating a call for the NFL to put an end to the use of facial recognition in football stadiums, where the groups say the technology is used to authenticate employees, vendors, and author
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: AI lifts workforces to new heights of efficiency and innovation
item.description: AI is a highly effective, multifaceted tool that can not only improve worker productivity for simple tasks, but can also dramatically transform customer service, cybersecurity, and even business strategy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump gives vote of confidence in Musk on DOGE
item.description: President Trump on Friday gave a vote of confidence in tech entrepreneur Elon Musk’s work on the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), saying that his billionaire ally is doing a “very good job.” “Elon is doing a great job. He’s finding tremendous fraud and corruption and waste.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Academic researchers are going to have to learn algospeak
item.description: Imagine you’re an academic researcher. You’re writing a pitch for funding to the National Science Foundation (NSF), the independent agency of the federal government that funds projects designed to advance our understanding of the world.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk team’s access to student loan systems raises alarms over personal information for millions
item.description: A lawsuit filed seeks to block DOGE’s access to student financial aid systems, saying it violates privacy rights of millions of federal student loan borrowers. It was filed by the advocacy group Student Defense on behalf of the University of California Student Association.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Transportation Department suspends $5 billion EV charger program
item.description: The Federal Highway Administration (FHA) on Thursday announced the suspension of a Biden-era electric vehicle (EV) charging network, the latest in a series of moves by the Trump administration to gut its predecessors’ energy and environmental moves.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, The Hill

item.title: Musk asks X if he should rehire DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk took to his social media platform X to ask users if he should rehire one of his deputies who resigned Thursday after racist social media posts of his resurfaced.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: AWS plans to outspend Microsoft and Google on on AI infrastructure
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) intends to invest $100 billion in ramping up infrastructure for AI cloud services this year, surpassing the spending plans of rivals Microsoft and Google.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bay Area companies Salesforce, Workday are hiring more overseas to save cash
item.description: Software companies are under pressure to invest in new artificial intelligence capabilities without denting profits. One increasingly popular strategy to keep costs low is to shift hiring outside the US.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Sorry, you're not getting Microsoft Edge off of your PC, at least according to its new 'uninstall' document
item.description: When Microsoft tells you to use its Edge browser, it means it, as an uninstall guide literally doesn't instruct you on how to do it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: My new favorite thing in EVs? This Citroen Ami that's been made to look like a 1950s H-Van
item.description: Caselani takes modern Citroens and clads them in fiberglass so they look just like iconic 1940s vans.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The number of venomous snakes removed from this Australian yard will make you shudder
item.description: A man described feeling “the shudders” as more than 100 venomous red-bellied black snakes were removed from a pile of mulch in his Sydney backyard.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones under a new law restricting the use of devices in schools.
item.dc:creator: Gabriela Sá Pessoa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI takes big step forward with 3D-printed shoe
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Quantum networking advances on Earth and in space
item.description: Cisco’s Quantum Summit brought together quantum computing experts last week from around academia, government, and industry and highlighted the progress we’ve seen over the past few months.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: I’m a tech expert: 10 AI prompts you’ll use all the time
item.description: From proofreading and research to brainstorming and data analysis, you can use generative AI as a sidekick for just about any task you can dream of.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
